title: Ctenopteris trifurcata
taxonomy: Ctenopteris trifurcata is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, and further classified into the division Pteridophyta. As a member of the family Dryopteridaceae, Ctenopteris trifurcata is closely related to species such as Elaphoglossum and Dryopteris. The genus Ctenopteris is part of the larger family of ferns, which also includes the genera Aspidium and Polystichum. This species of fern is native to Australia and New Zealand, where it is found in shaded, humid environments.
uses: Ctenopteris trifurcata is a plant native to New Zealand, and while there is limited information on its medicinal uses, some studies suggest that it has potential health benefits. Traditionally, the plant has been used by the indigenous Māori people for medicinal purposes, but the exact applications are not well-documented. parts: Ctenopteris trifurcata, a species of fern native to New Zealand, has been used in traditional Maori medicine.
